risk_title: Political and Governance Risk - Substantial Rating Maintained
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain rated as Substantial throughout project implementation, indicating persistent fragility in the institutional and political environment affecting project execution in Sindh Province.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Rating at Approval: Substantial, Last Approved Rating: Substantial

risk_title: Scope Reduction Due to Government Policy Shift on Emergency Activities
risk_summary: Project scope was significantly reduced when the Government of Sindh agreed in April 2021 that the project would not finance emergency cleaning activities (drainage channel cleaning, waste collection from nullahs, and PPE/training for COVID-19 mitigation), despite these being part of the original emergency response component. This indicates governance tensions and shifting political priorities affecting project implementation.
evidence_quote: While the Government of Sindh reported removing 300,000 metric tons of waste from drains prior to the monsoon season in 2020, the subsequent agreement that the project will not finance these expenditures means that these results cannot be attributed to the project.

risk_summary: Institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability is rated as Substantial risk, suggesting ongoing challenges with the implementing agency (Local Government Department, Province of Sindh) in executing project activities effectively.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability - Rating at Approval: Substantial, Last Approved Rating: Substantial
